content.thinking:
The exploration is complete. The bug is clear and simple. Let me now write the plan file and then exit plan mode.

The bug is on line 7 of `src/utils.ts`:
```typescript
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
```
Should be:
```typescript
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

Let me write the plan file.

input.content:
# Bug Fix Plan: `processItems` off-by-one error

## Context
Users report that the last item in the array passed to `processItems` is never included in the
output. The root cause is an off-by-one error in the loop condition: `i < items.length - 1`
stops one iteration early, so the final element is always skipped.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/utils.ts` | Contains the buggy `processItems` function (line 7) |
| `tests/utils.test.ts` | Existing tests that cover the fix |

---

## The Bug

**`src/utils.ts`, line 7:**
```typescript
// BUGGY — stops one index short of the last element
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
```

---

## Fix (single-line change)

Change the loop condition from `items.length - 1` to `items.length`:

```typescript
// FIXED
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

No other changes are needed.

---

## Verification

Run the test suite with:
```
npm test
```
(`vitest run` per `package.json`)

All four `processItems` tests should now pass:
- `should process all items in the array` — was failing (last item skipped)
- `should handle a single item` — was failing (loop never ran)
- `should handle two items` — was failing (only first item processed)
- `should return empty array for empty input` — was already passing
